description: Established strength with declining demand or meaning momentum.

all.evidence: Demand Power is category-leading, so the brand is still structurally strong.
all.missingEvidence: Demand Power is not category-leading, so the strong-but-slipping rule should not be primary.

any.evidence: Demand Power momentum is declining.
any.missingEvidence: Demand Power momentum is not declining.

any.evidence: Meaningful momentum is declining, pointing to relevance erosion.

counter.evidence: Demand Power is category-lagging, which argues against a strong-base diagnosis.

evidenceSummary: The brand has a strong demand base, but demand or relevance momentum is slipping.

description: Memory or scale exists, but Difference and/or Pricing Power are not carrying enough choice power.

all.evidence: Salience is not category-lagging, so the brand has a usable memory base.
all.missingEvidence: Salience is category-lagging, which makes generic salience less likely than a broader foundation issue.

any.evidence: Different is not ahead of the benchmark, so the reason to choose may be underpowered.
any.missingEvidence: Different is ahead, which weakens the generic-salience read.

any.evidence: Different is category-lagging, indicating a weak point of difference.
Pricing Power is not ahead, suggesting familiarity may not be translating into price justification.

counter.evidence: Different is ahead, which complicates a generic-salience diagnosis.

evidenceSummary: The brand has enough memory presence to work with, but Difference and/or Pricing Power are not strong enough.

description: Relevance is strong, but Salience is weaker than the relevance base.

all.evidence: Meaningful is category-leading, so relevance is a strength.
all.missingEvidence: Meaningful is not category-leading, so the brand may need relevance work before salience work.

any.evidence: Salience is not category-leading, so mental availability trails relevance.
Salience is not ahead of the benchmark.

counter.evidence: Meaningful is not ahead, which means relevance may not be strong enough to call the brand merely invisible.

evidenceSummary: The brand appears relevant to consumers who know it, but needs stronger mental availability.

description: Difference is the standout strength, but Meaningful or Salient lag.

all.evidence: Different is category-leading, so the brand has a distinctive edge.

any.evidence: Meaningful is category-lagging, so the difference may not yet be broadly relevant.
Salience is category-lagging, so the difference is not yet easy to recall.
Demand Power is category-lagging despite high Difference, indicating niche distinctiveness.

counter.evidence: Meaningful is category-leading, which suggests the difference may already be broadly relevant.

evidenceSummary: Difference is a real asset, but the brand has not fully translated it into broad relevance, salience, or demand.

description: Multiple core equity metrics are category-lagging, indicating foundational repair before optimization.

all.evidence: Demand Power is not ahead, so the brand does not have a clear demand advantage.

any.evidence: Demand Power is category-lagging.
Meaningful is category-lagging.
Different is category-lagging.
Salient is category-lagging.

counter.evidence: Demand Power is category-leading, which argues against a foundation deficit.

evidenceSummary: The brand is weak across enough foundational equity signals that basic brand-building should come before optimization.

description: Demand Power is below ambition and at least two M/D/S inputs are not ahead or category-lagging.

all.evidence: Demand Power is not ahead, so scaled growth expectations need caution.

any.evidence: Meaningful is not ahead.
Different is not ahead.
Salience is not ahead.
Demand Power is category-lagging.

counter.evidence: Demand Power is category-leading, which means this is not a broad underbuilt-equity read.

evidenceSummary: Demand Power is below ambition and multiple equity inputs are not doing enough work.

description: Pricing Power is not ahead, with weak Difference or Meaningful support.

all.evidence: Pricing Power is not ahead, so broad equity price justification needs attention.

any.evidence: Different is not ahead, limiting distinct price justification.
Meaningful is not ahead, limiting relevance-based price justification.
Pricing Power is category-lagging.

counter.evidence: Pricing Power is ahead, which argues against a price-value vulnerability.

evidenceSummary: Pricing Power is not supported strongly enough by the current equity pattern.

description: No major ailment: core demand, pricing, and M/D/S signals are ahead or at least not category-lagging.

any.evidence: Demand Power is ahead of the benchmark.
Pricing Power is ahead of the benchmark.
Meaningful is ahead of the benchmark.
Different is ahead of the benchmark.
Salience is ahead of the benchmark.

counter.evidence: Demand Power momentum is declining, so monitor status needs caution.
Meaningful momentum is declining, so monitor status needs caution.

evidenceSummary: No high-priority ailment fires from the current rule set; maintain and monitor.
